note: Hand-entered one-off events for Honolulu / Oahu metro to populate the weekend guide.

events.title: Honolulu Zoo Family Adventure Tour
events.description: A special guided walking tour of the zoo for family groups. Learn about zoo animals, their habitats, and conservation efforts with zoo educators.

events.title: Bishop Museum Keiki Science Day
events.description: Interactive science labs and hands-on demonstrations designed for curious kids and families. Explore the wonders of science and Hawaiian natural history.

events.title: Waikiki Aquarium Reef Exploration
events.description: Join aquarium educators for a guided exploration of the shallow reef flats in Waikiki. Discover crabs, sea stars, and other reef life in their natural environment.

events.title: Ala Moana Beach Family Sandcastle Building Contest
events.description: Annual community sandcastle building contest at Ala Moana Beach. Bring your buckets and shovels. Prizes for various age groups and categories!

events.title: Hawaii Children's Discovery Center Art & Craft Studio
events.description: Creative hands-on art and crafting session in the discovery center studio. Let your child's imagination run wild with recycled materials and colors.

events.title: Kailua Independence Day Parade 2026 — Free July 4th Family Parade on Oahu
events.description: Kick off Independence Day at Kailua's free July 4th parade marching down North Kainalu Drive on Oahu's windward side. This all-ages community celebration is a beloved Kailua tradition with floats, marching units, and patriotic family fun. It's the official Oahu signature event of the Hawai'i America250 Commission.

events.title: Kailua Beach 4th of July Fireworks 2026 — Free July 4th Show on Oahu
events.description: Cap off the Fourth of July with Kailua's legendary fireworks show over Kailua Beach, a free family tradition on Oahu's windward coast. Pack a beach blanket and watch the night sky light up at 8:00 PM. Free shuttle service runs from the Longs parking lot so keiki and families can enjoy the show stress-free.

events.title: Hilo Hot Rides Expo & Live Music 2026 — Free July 4th Family Fun on Hilo Bayfront
events.description: Celebrate July 4th on the Hilo Bayfront with the free Hot Rides Expo, featuring vintage, classic, and custom cars plus live local music. The Hawai‘i County Band performs at the Mo‘oheau Park Bandstand, making it a fun, free daytime outing for the whole family before the evening fireworks.

events.title: Hilo Bay Blast Fireworks 2026 — Free July 4th Fireworks Over Hilo Bay
events.description: Watch the Hilo Bay Blast light up the night over Hilo Bay this Fourth of July, with professional fireworks launched from a barge at 8:00 PM. This free, all-ages celebration draws families to the Bayfront for one of the Big Island's biggest July 4th fireworks displays.

events.title: Kauai Hospice Concert in the Sky 2026 — Lihue July 4th Music & Fireworks Festival
events.description: Kauai's largest July 4th celebration returns to Vidinha Stadium in Lihue for the 33rd Annual Kauai Hospice Concert in the Sky. Families enjoy live island music, food vendors, local crafts, a keiki fun zone, and a spectacular fireworks finale. Keiki under 13 get in free at this beloved community fundraiser.

events.title: Pearl Harbor Aviation Museum July 4th 2026 America250 Celebration — Honolulu
events.description: Celebrate America's 250th birthday at the Pearl Harbor Aviation Museum on historic Ford Island this Fourth of July. The public is invited to this family-friendly, 90-minute patriotic program inside the iconic World War II hangar in Honolulu.

events.title: Field of Flags at Hawai‘i State Capitol 2026 — Free July 4th Patriotic Display in Honolulu
events.description: Visit the free Field of Flags at the Hawai‘i State Capitol in downtown Honolulu, a patriotic display of 250 flags honoring America's 250th birthday through July 4th. It's a meaningful, all-ages stop for families looking to celebrate Independence Day.
